content: The block start is where most high school sprinters leave time on the track. We break down shin angle, arm drive, and the patience it takes to stay low through the first three steps instead of popping straight up.
excerpt: Cleaning up the block start and the first three steps.
title: Sprint mechanics: cleaning up the first three steps

content: Coming back from an ACL reconstruction is about more than getting cleared. Here is the criteria-based progression I use with athletes — hop testing, landing mechanics, and gradual sprint exposure — before they ever touch a starting block again.
excerpt: A criteria-based return after an ACL tear.
title: Returning to sprinting after an ACL tear

content: Talent gets you to the meet. Habits win the meet. Why I build our Crestwood program around boring fundamentals done consistently, and how that shows up on race day.
excerpt: Discipline over hype, every single practice.
title: My coaching philosophy: discipline over hype

content: From the Crestwood South loop at 5:15 AM to Vulcan Trail and the Lakeshore path, here are the Birmingham routes that get me through a marathon build, with notes on where to park and where the hills hide.
excerpt: The Birmingham routes I keep coming back to.
title: Birmingham running routes I keep coming back to

content: Smoked chicken on Sunday, rice and greens through Wednesday, and a real plate of BBQ on the weekend. How I fuel back-to-back early mornings without living on gas-station food.
excerpt: Meal prep for a coach who runs at dawn.
title: Meal prep for a coach who runs at dawn

content: Sprinters live and die by their hamstrings. The eccentric and tempo work I build into our warmups to keep athletes healthy and on the track all season.
excerpt: Prevention beats rehab for hamstrings.
title: Hamstring strains: prevention beats rehab

content: Why New Hope AME, the men's choir, and a quiet Sunday long run are the rhythm that keeps the rest of my week honest. A little about faith, and a little about the miles.
excerpt: Faith, the choir, and Sunday afternoon miles.
title: Faith, the choir, and running on Sunday afternoons

content: Following one of our D1 prospects through film, calls, and a December campus visit. What the recruiting process actually looks like for a Crestwood sprinter, and what I tell families to expect.
excerpt: Devon's unofficial visit and the recruiting grind.
title: Devon's recruiting journey: the unofficial visit

content: Twelve weeks of mileage, tempo work, and long runs leading into the Birmingham half. What worked, what I cut, and the splits I am chasing on Mercedes weekend.
excerpt: Building for the Mercedes-Benz weekend half.
title: Half marathon training log: building for Mercedes-Benz weekend

content: Demetrius here. The exchange zone is won in practice, not in the meet. How we drill the 4x100 handoff until it stops being a guessing game and starts being automatic.
excerpt: Drilling the 4x100 exchange zone.
title: Relay handoffs: the exchange zone is won in practice

content: Cold and windy, but the sprint group looked sharp. Devon opened in 10.8 into a headwind and the 4x100 ran clean exchanges. A full rundown of our season opener at Crestwood.
excerpt: Recap of the Crestwood track season opener.
title: Crestwood track season opener recap

content: Tough rolling course at Oak Mountain. The team packed up well through the middle mile and we moved three runners into the top twenty. Notes on the course and what we will fix before next year.
excerpt: Recap of the Cross Country Invitational at Oak Mountain.
title: Crestwood Cross Country Invitational recap

content: Ran the half through downtown and Highland Park on Mercedes weekend. Negative-split the back half and held it together up the last hill on 20th. Here is how the race went, mile by mile.
excerpt: My Mercedes-Benz Marathon weekend half recap.
title: Mercedes-Benz Marathon weekend half recap

content: Devon Mitchell is a senior, our team captain, and a real D1 prospect with bests of 10.6 and 21.4. A look at how far he has come and what makes him a leader on this team.
excerpt: Spotlight on senior sprinter Devon Mitchell.
title: Athlete spotlight: Devon Mitchell

content: Trey Dawson is a junior quarter-miler who anchors our 4x400 and dropped two seconds off his open 400 over the winter. A look at his work ethic and where he is headed.
excerpt: Spotlight on junior 400 runner Trey Dawson.
title: Athlete spotlight: Trey Dawson

content: Easy 10 at 8:05 pace from Lakeshore to Vulcan. Legs felt heavy the first three miles, then settled in. Cold start, sunny finish, good Saturday.

title: Running log: Saturday long run, Lakeshore to Vulcan

content: A twelve-week speed block I use with our high school sprinters in the off-season. Acceleration, max velocity, and the rest that makes it all stick.
excerpt: An off-season speed development plan for sprinters.

content: What I tell young athletes about eating to train and recover. Real food, enough of it, and timing it around practice instead of chasing supplements.

title: Fueling young athletes: real food first

content: Early notes on our spring relay handoff clinic — two days of nothing but exchanges. Still working out the schedule before I open registration.

content: A guest note from Demetrius on managing shin splints in young runners — load, footwear, and when to back off before it becomes a stress reaction.
